metadata.scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for students with Disabilities - DNH & DD
metadata.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

raw_data.details: The scheme “Scholarships for Students with Disabilities” has been launched by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India, and aims to provide scholarship assistance and educational support to students with benchmark disabilities pursuing Post-Matric education. Through this scheme, financial assistance, tuition fee support, maintenance allowance, and related benefits are provided to eligible students with disabilities studying from Class 11 up to Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma level.
raw_data.benefits: Scholarship support is available for studies from Class 11 up to Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma level. Eligible courses should be approved by recognised bodies such as:University Grants Commission (UGC)National Medical Commission (NMC)Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)State Government or Union Territory GovernmentBenefits for Employed/Intern Students:Employed or intern students whose combined income with parents/guardians does not exceed the prescribed income ceiling are eligible for all components of the Post-Matric Scholarship. Tuition Fee/Coaching Fee:Tuition fee or coaching fee is released to the Implementing Agency through a Central Nodal Agency (CNA). Maintenance Allowance and Disability Allowance:Maintenance allowance and disability allowance are released through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the Aadhaar-enabled bank account of the candidate. A minimum of 70% monthly attendance is required for eligibility for stipend, maintenance allowance, and disability allowance for a particular month. Book Allowance:Book allowance is released in advance to candidates meeting 50% attendance during the first 10 days. Other eligible candidates receive the allowance with the first instalment of maintenance/disability allowance, provided they continue with the course. Mode of Disbursement:Tuition fee/coaching fee is released through the Central Nodal Agency (CNA) to the Implementing Agency. Stipend and admissible allowances are released through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). Frequency of DisbursementTuition Fee/Coaching Fee:First instalment: 50% advance release based on candidates meeting 50% attendance during the first 10 days. Second instalment: Released on completion of the course, considering candidates attending beyond the halfway mark. Book Allowance:Released in advance or with the first instalment of maintenance/disability allowance. Validity/Conditions:Minimum 70% monthly attendance is compulsory for stipend and allowance eligibility. Aadhaar Number and Unique Disability ID (UDID) are mandatory. Benefits are subject to scheme guidelines and verification procedures.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a student with a benchmark disability of 40% or more as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The applicant should possess a Unique Disability ID (UDID). The applicant should possess an Aadhaar Card. The applicant should pursue eligible Post-Matric educational courses covered under the scheme. The applicant should satisfy the prescribed income ceiling criteria. The applicant should study in Class 11 to Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma level. The applicant should pursue courses approved by recognized bodies such as:University Grants Commission (UGC)State Government or Union Territory GovernmentNational Medical Commission (NMC)Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 1: Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents. Go to http://www.scholarships.gov.in/. and click “New Registration”. Guidelines for Registration will appear. Scroll to the bottom. Read carefully the undertaking. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”.
Step 2: A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory)Fill the details and click “Register”. Your Application ID and password will be displayed. The same will also be sent as an SMS on your registered mobile number.
Step 3: Go to https://scholarships.gov.in/fresh/newstdRegfrmInstruction Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password. Type the Captcha and click “Login”. On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen. Create a new password and confirm. Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
Step 4: On the left pane, click “Application Form”. The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill the details and upload the documents. You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later. Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.
raw_data.documents_required: Photograph. Proof of Age. Certificate of Disability (Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016). Income Certificate. Tuition Fee Receipt. Last academic qualification certificate. Bank Details of the applicant or of the Parent/Guardian.

Certificate of Disability (Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016). Income Certificate. Tuition Fee Receipt. Last academic qualification certificate. Bank Details of the applicant or of the Parent/Guardian. Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the objective of the scheme? The scheme aims to provide scholarship assistance and educational support to students with benchmark disabilities pursuing Post-Matric education from Class 11 to Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma level. Which department implements the scheme? The scheme is implemented by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India. Who can apply for the Post-Matric Scholarship? Students with benchmark disabilities of 40% or more, as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, can apply for the scholarship subject to prescribed eligibility conditions. What educational courses are covered under the scheme? The scheme covers Class 11, Diploma, Undergraduate, Postgraduate Degree, and Diploma courses approved by recognized bodies such as the University Grants Commission (UGC), National Medical Commission (NMC), Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI), and All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). Is Unique Disability ID (UDID) mandatory for the scheme? Yes. The applicant should possess a valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) to apply under the scheme. Can employed or intern students apply for the scholarship? Yes. Employed or intern students are eligible if their combined income with parents/guardians does not exceed the prescribed income ceiling. How is the scholarship amount disbursed to students? Stipend and admissible allowances are released through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the Aadhaar-enabled bank account of the candidate. Is attendance mandatory for receiving benefits under the scheme? Yes. A minimum 70% monthly attendance is required for receiving stipend, maintenance allowance, and disability allowance for a particular month. Can a student studying in another State apply for the scholarship? Yes. If the applicant is a permanent resident of one State but studying in another State, the application should be recommended by the Education/Welfare Department of the State where the student is studying. Where can applicants apply for the scholarship? Applicants can apply online through the National Scholarship Portal by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and submitting the application online. Sources And References[NSP Portal](https://scholarships.gov.in/)
[Guidelines](https://cdnbbsr.s3waas.gov.in/s3e58aea67b01fa747687f038dfde066f6/uploads/2025/09/202509111852335844.pdf)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide scholarship assistance and educational support to students with benchmark disabilities pursuing Post-Matric education from Class 11 to Post-Graduate Degree or Diploma level.

faqs.question: Which department implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the Post-Matric Scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students with benchmark disabilities of 40% or more, as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, can apply for the scholarship subject to prescribed eligibility conditions.

faqs.question: What educational courses are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers Class 11, Diploma, Undergraduate, Postgraduate Degree, and Diploma courses approved by recognized bodies such as the University Grants Commission (UGC), National Medical Commission (NMC), Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI), and All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).

faqs.question: Is Unique Disability ID (UDID) mandatory for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes. The applicant should possess a valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: Can employed or intern students apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes. Employed or intern students are eligible if their combined income with parents/guardians does not exceed the prescribed income ceiling.

faqs.question: How is the scholarship amount disbursed to students?
faqs.answer: Stipend and admissible allowances are released through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the Aadhaar-enabled bank account of the candidate.

faqs.question: Is attendance mandatory for receiving benefits under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes. A minimum 70% monthly attendance is required for receiving stipend, maintenance allowance, and disability allowance for a particular month.

faqs.question: Can a student studying in another State apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes. If the applicant is a permanent resident of one State but studying in another State, the application should be recommended by the Education/Welfare Department of the State where the student is studying.

faqs.question: Where can applicants apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Applicants can apply online through the National Scholarship Portal by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and submitting the application online.
